questions.question: What does this PDO code output if the query returns one row with name 'Alice'?
questions.codeSnippet:
<?php
$pdo = new PDO('mysql:host=localhost;dbname=test', 'user', 'pass');
$stmt = $pdo->query("SELECT name FROM users WHERE id = 1");
$row = $stmt->fetch(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
echo $row['name'];
?>
questions.explanation: The fetch returns an associative array, so $row['name'] contains 'Alice'.

questions.question: What will this prepared statement output?
questions.codeSnippet:
<?php
$pdo = new PDO('mysql:host=localhost;dbname=test', 'user', 'pass');
$stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > :age");
$stmt->execute([':age' => 18]);
echo $stmt->rowCount();
?>
questions.explanation: `rowCount()` returns the number of rows affected/selected by the last statement.

questions.question: Given this JSON, what does the code output?
questions.explanation: `json_decode` without second parameter returns an object. `$obj->name` gives "John".

questions.question: What will this cURL snippet do?
questions.codeSnippet:
<?php
$ch = curl_init();
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, "https://api.example.com/data");
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, 1);
$response = curl_exec($ch);
curl_close($ch);
echo $response;
?>
questions.explanation: This fetches the URL and returns the response as a string, then prints it.
